app.name: KSail
app.one_liner: KSail is a tool that bundles common Kubernetes tooling into a single binary, offering CLI, VSCode Extension, AI-Enabled Chat TUI, and MCP interfaces to create, deploy, and operate cloud-native stacks across distributions and providers.

screens.name: Cluster Dashboard
screens.purpose: Display available clusters, their status, and quick actions for deployment and operations.

rust_commands.name: create_cluster
rust_commands.purpose: Provision a Kubernetes cluster using native configs for Kind, K3d, Talos, or VCluster on supported providers.

rust_commands.name: deploy_workload
rust_commands.purpose: Apply GitOps manifests (Flux/ArgoCD) or Helm/Kustomize specs to a target cluster.

rust_commands.name: manage_secrets
rust_commands.purpose: Encrypt, decrypt, and edit secrets using built-in SOPS integration.

mvp_plan: week 1: Build basic TUI interface for cluster creation and status viewing
week 1: Implement core cluster provisioning logic for Docker-based Kind and K3d
week 1: Integrate SOPS for secret management
acceptance_tests: User can create a Kind cluster on Docker using a kind.yaml file and verify it appears in the dashboard.
User can deploy a Helm chart to a K3d cluster via the CLI and confirm successful reconciliation.
User can encrypt a secret using SOPS through the manage_secrets command and decrypt it back correctly.
